content: I'll decompose this design bead into focused child beads that can be tackled independently. Let me create the children and wire the dependencies.

args.command:
br create "Define first-failure state data structure" "
## Child of: adc-4vhr

## Task
Define the data structure for tracking first-failure state after Telegram send startup.

## Scope
- Design the state structure (what fields are needed)
- Define types (bool, timestamp, counter, etc.)
- Consider what metadata is useful (when, which channel, error type)

## Acceptance Criteria
- State structure documented with fields and types
- Explains what each field tracks
- Considers reset scenarios (startup, manual reset)
" --label split-child
args.description: Create first child bead for data structure

args.command:
br create --title "Define first-failure state data structure" --description "
## Child of: adc-4vhr

## Task
Define the data structure for tracking first-failure state after Telegram send startup.

## Scope
- Design the state structure (what fields are needed)
- Define types (bool, timestamp, counter, etc.)
- Consider what metadata is useful (when, which channel, error type)

## Acceptance Criteria
- State structure documented with fields and types
- Explains what each field tracks
- Considers reset scenarios (startup, manual reset)
" --label split-child
args.description: Create first child bead for data structure

output: adc-65l3

args.command:
br create --title "Design state storage mechanism for async FastAPI" --description "
## Child of: adc-4vhr

## Task
Design WHERE the first-failure state lives in the async FastAPI application.

## Scope
- Evaluate storage options (module-level var, class attribute, request context, database)
- Consider lifecycle (when it initializes, when it resets)
- Consider persistence requirements (does it survive restarts?)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Storage location documented with rationale
- Explains initialization timing
- Explains reset behavior (startup vs manual)
- Depends on child bead adc-65l3 completing the data structure design
" --label split-child
args.description: Create second child bead for storage mechanism

output: adc-2duz

args.command:
br create --title "Design thread-safety approach for async FastAPI" --description "
## Child of: adc-4vhr

## Task
Design how to safely read/write first-failure state across concurrent requests in async FastAPI.

## Scope
- Identify race conditions (multiple sends failing simultaneously)
- Evaluate locking mechanisms (asyncio.Lock, module-level mutex, atomic operations)
- Consider performance implications of locking

## Acceptance Criteria
- Thread-safety approach documented
- Identifies specific race conditions to prevent
- Explains locking strategy (if any) or why not needed
- Depends on child bead adc-2duz completing storage mechanism design
" --label split-child
args.description: Create third child bead for thread-safety

output: adc-50ld

args.command:
br create --title "Design first-failure detection logic" --description "
## Child of: adc-4vhr

## Task
Design HOW to detect the first failure vs subsequent failures and trigger notification only once.

## Scope
- Design the check logic (if not already failed: notify and mark)
- Define when to check (before send, after failure, both)
- Consider edge cases (intermittent failures, config changes)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Detection logic documented with clear pseudo-code or flow
- Explains how \"first\" is determined
- Explains why subsequent failures are ignored
- Depends on child bead adc-50ld completing thread-safety design
" --label split-child
args.description: Create fourth child bead for detection logic

output: adc-12bt

args.command:
br create --title "Document complete first-failure tracking design" --description "
## Child of: adc-4vhr

## Task
Synthesize all prior child beads into a complete design document for first-failure tracking.

## Scope
- Integrate: data structure (adc-65l3) + storage (adc-2duz) + thread-safety (adc-50ld) + detection logic (adc-12bt)
- Provide end-to-end flow description
- Include implementation guidance for the next bead (implementation)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Complete design documented in this bead body
- All components integrated coherently
- Clear implementation guidance for next phase
- Depends on child bead adc-12bt completing detection logic design
" --label split-child
args.description: Create fifth child bead for complete design

output: adc-14la

content:
SPLIT_COMPLETE: Created 5 children, parent converted to umbrella

Children: adc-65l3, adc-2duz, adc-50ld, adc-12bt, adc-14la

**Chain Summary:**
1. **adc-65l3** — Define first-failure state data structure
2. **adc-2duz** — Design state storage mechanism for async FastAPI *(blocks on adc-65l3)*
3. **adc-50ld** — Design thread-safety approach for async FastAPI *(blocks on adc-2duz)*
4. **adc-12bt** — Design first-failure detection logic *(blocks on adc-50ld)*
5. **adc-14la** — Document complete first-failure tracking design *(blocks on adc-12bt)*

**Parent (adc-4vhr):** Now an umbrella bead with `umbrella` label, depends on last child (adc-14la)
